title: Remote Virus & Malware Removal
headline: Remove threats without anyone stepping foot inside.
description: Remote malware cleanup for enrolled devices when secure access is already in place.
metaTitle: Remote Virus & Malware Removal for Central Florida Plan Members | Defend I.T. Solutions
metaDescription: Remote malware cleanup for Central Florida Remote Support Plan members, including spyware, browser hijacks, fake antivirus tools, and post-cleanup guidance to reduce reinfection.

sections.heading: Did You Know?
paragraph: Modern threats like spyware, keyloggers, and fileless malware often avoid detection by traditional antivirus tools.
Some infections survive resets and reinstalls by embedding into startup tasks, boot sectors, or profile data.
Remote cleanup using professional tools can be just as effective as an in-person service when done correctly and securely.

sections.heading: What We Remove
items: Fake antivirus tools and system bloatware
Keyloggers, clipboard grabbers, and tracking agents
Rootkits and persistence-based malware that resist standard removal methods

sections.heading: How It Works
paragraph: If the device can still be reached safely, we can clean up many common infections remotely and then close the settings or habits that helped cause the problem.
During your initial setup visit, we install a secure access tool with your consent. Once active, you can request help anytime, and we clean your system remotely.
You stay in control throughout the process. Every session requires your approval, and you can watch live or disconnect at any time.

faq.question: Do I need a Remote Support Plan first?
faq.answer: Yes. Remote virus removal is available only to clients who already have an active Remote Support Plan and secure remote access in place.

faq.question: Can all malware be removed remotely?
faq.answer: Many common infections can be handled remotely, but some severe cases may require deeper recovery steps or a different approach depending on the condition of the system.

faq.question: Do you help reduce reinfection after cleanup?
faq.answer: Yes. Cleanup is only part of the service. We also review safer settings and next steps to help keep the same problem from happening again.
